originalTitle: 3 Big Reasons to Love Nicolet Bankshares (NIC)
summary: Since July 2021, the S&P 500 has delivered a total return of 74.3%. But one standout stock has nearly doubled the market - over the past five years, Nicolet Bankshares has surged 132% to $165.54 per share. Its momentum hasn’t stopped as it’s also gained 25.9% in the last six months thanks to its solid quarterly results, beating the S&P by 17.2%.
content:
Since July 2021, the S&P 500 has delivered a total return of 74.3%. But one standout stock has nearly doubled the market - over the past five years, Nicolet Bankshares has surged 132% to $165.54 per share. Its momentum hasn't stopped as it's also gained 25.9% in the last six months thanks to its solid quarterly results, beating the S&P by 17.2%.

What drove Nicolet Bankshares' 132% five-year stock surge?

What geographic markets does Nicolet Bankshares serve primarily?

Why is Nicolet's tangible book value growth significant?

How has Nicolet's net interest margin expansion impacted performance?

Following the strength, is NIC a buy right now? Or is the market overestimating its value? Find out in our full research report, it's free .

Why Is Nicolet Bankshares a Good Business?

Starting as Green Bay Financial Corporation in 2000 before rebranding in 2002, Nicolet Bankshares (NYSE:NIC) is a regional bank holding company that provides commercial, agricultural, and consumer banking services primarily in Wisconsin, Michigan, and Minnesota.

1. Net Interest Income Skyrockets, Fueling Growth Opportunities

Net interest income commands greater market attention due to its reliability and consistency, whereas one-time fees are often seen as lower-quality revenue that lacks the same dependable characteristics.

Nicolet Bankshares's net interest income has grown at a 21.9% annualized rate over the last five years, much better than the broader banking industry and faster than its total revenue.

2. Increasing Net Interest Margin Juices Financials

The net interest margin (NIM) is a key profitability indicator that measures the difference between what a bank earns on its loans and what it pays on its deposits. This metric measures how efficiently it can generate income from its core lending activities.

Over the past two years, Nicolet Bankshares's net interest margin averaged 3.6%, climbing by 65 basis points (100 basis points = 1 percentage point) over that period.

This expansion was a tailwind for its net interest income, and while prevailing interest rates matter the most for industry net interest margins, banks that consistently increase this figure generally boast higher-earning loan books (all else equal such as the risk of those loans) or provide differentiated services that give them the ability to charge higher rates (pricing power).

3. Growing TBVPS Reflects Strong Asset Base

In the banking industry, tangible book value per share (TBVPS) provides the clearest picture of shareholder value, as it focuses on concrete assets while excluding intangible items that may not hold value during challenging times.

Nicolet Bankshares's TBVPS increased by 10.2% annually over the last five years, and growth has recently accelerated as TBVPS grew at an excellent 17% annual clip over the past two years (from $43.28 to $59.21 per share).

These are just a few reasons Nicolet Bankshares is a rock-solid business worth owning, and with its shares beating the market recently, the stock trades at 1.5× forward P/B (or $165.54 per share). chineseTitle: 喜爱 Nicolet Bankshares (NIC) 的 3 大理由
chineseSummary: 自2021年7月以来，标准普尔500指数的总回报率为74.3%。但一只表现出色的股票的市值几乎翻了一番——在过去五年里，Nicolet Bankshares 飙升了 132%，达到每股 165.54 美元。它的势头并没有停止，由于其稳健的季度业绩，它在过去六个月中也上涨了 25.9%，比标准普尔指数高出 17.2%。
chineseContent:
自2021年7月以来，标准普尔500指数的总回报率为74.3%。但一只表现出色的股票的市值几乎翻了一番——在过去五年里，Nicolet Bankshares 飙升了 132%，达到每股 165.54 美元。它的势头并没有停止，由于其稳健的季度业绩，它在过去六个月中也上涨了 25.9%，比标准普尔指数高出 17.2%。

是什么推动 Nicolet Bankshares 股价五年内飙升 132%？

Nicolet Bankshares 主要服务于哪些地理市场？

为什么 Nicolet 的有形账面价值增长显着？

Nicolet 的净息差扩张对业绩有何影响？

跟随力量，NIC 现在值得买入吗？还是市场高估了它的价值？请在我们的完整研究报告中找到答案，它是免费的。

为什么 Nicolet Bankshares 是一项好生意？

Nicolet Bankshares (NYSE:NIC) 成立于 2000 年，前身为 Green Bay Financial Corporation，于 2002 年进行品牌重塑，是一家区域性银行控股公司，主要在威斯康星州、密歇根州和明尼苏达州提供商业、农业和消费者银行服务。

1.净利息收入飙升，推动增长机会

净利息收入因其可靠性和一致性而受到更多市场关注，而一次性费用通常被视为缺乏同样可靠特征的低质量收入。

Nicolet Bankshares 的净利息收入在过去五年中以年化 21.9% 的速度增长，远好于整个银行业，也快于其总收入。

2.净息差的增加有利于金融业

净息差（NIM）是一个关键的盈利指标，衡量银行的贷款收入与存款支付之间的差额。该指标衡量其从核心贷款活动中产生收入的效率。

过去两年，Nicolet Bankshares 的净息差平均为 3.6%，期间攀升了 65 个基点（100 个基点 = 1 个百分点）。

这种扩张是其净利息收入的顺风车，虽然现行利率对行业净息差影响最大，但持续增加这一数字的银行通常拥有更高收入的贷款账簿（其他条件相同，例如这些贷款的风险）或提供差异化​​服务，使他们有能力收取更高的利率（定价能力）。

3. 不断增长的 TBVPS 反映出强大的资产基础

在银行业，每股有形账面价值（TBVPS）提供了最清晰的股东价值图景，因为它专注于具体资产，同时排除在困难时期可能不保值的无形项目。

Nicolet Bankshares 的 TBVPS 在过去五年中每年增长 10.2%，而且最近增长速度有所加快，过去两年中 TBVPS 的年增长率高达 17%（从每股 43.28 美元增至 59.21 美元）。

这些只是 Nicolet Bankshares 是一家值得拥有的坚如磐石的企业的几个原因，而且由于其股价最近跑赢了市场，该股的交易价格为 1.5 倍远期市净率（或每股 165.54 美元）。现在是开始建仓的好时机吗？请亲自查看我们的深入研究报告，它是免费的。

适合所有市场条件的优质股票

另外值得关注的是：前 5 名动量股票。持有优质股票的最佳时机是当市场最终注意到它时。这些不仅仅是高质量的企业。他们现在正在发生一些事情。精英基本面与近期势头相结合——这两个方框同时被选中。
